metadata.name: WordPress Core Execution Tests - Rewrite and Permalinks
metadata.description: Code generation tasks for WordPress Rewrite and Permalinks APIs

tests.prompt: Provide a PHP function named wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_001(): void that registers item URLs under items/<item-slug> as an extra permalink structure using a %wpbp_item% placeholder.
tests.expected_behavior: Reviewer contract: The helper must register the %wpbp_item% rewrite tag, expose the wpbp_item query variable, and add a wpbp_item permastruct whose structure contains items/%wpbp_item%. Review focus: Use WordPress rewrite APIs and do not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime. The runtime inspects WordPress rewrite state after calling the helper rather than trusting its return value.
requirements: Avoid flushing rewrite rules during normal runtime

required_patterns.description: Registers the requested item permalink structure

forbidden_patterns.description: Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime
Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_001' ) ) { return false; } global $wp, $wp_rewrite; wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_001(); $tag_index = array_search( '%wpbp_item%', $wp_rewrite->rewritecode, true ); if ( false === $tag_index ) { return false; } $regex = $wp_rewrite->rewritereplace[ $tag_index ] ?? ''; $pattern = '~^' . str_replace( '~', '\\~', $regex ) . '$~'; $accepts_slug = 1 === @preg_match( $pattern, 'alpha-1' ); $rejects_nested_path = 1 !== @preg_match( $pattern, 'alpha/beta' ); $query_replace = $wp_rewrite->queryreplace[ $tag_index ] ?? ''; $struct = $wp_rewrite->extra_permastructs['wpbp_item']['struct'] ?? ''; return $accepts_slug && $rejects_nested_path && 'wpbp_item=' === $query_replace && in_array( 'wpbp_item', $wp->public_query_vars, true ) && str_contains( $struct, 'items/%wpbp_item%' );
assertions.description: The helper registers the item rewrite tag, query var, and permastruct in WordPress

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_001(): void { add_rewrite_tag( '%wpbp_item%', '([^/]+)' ); add_permastruct( 'wpbp_item', 'items/%wpbp_item%', array( 'with_front' => false ) ); }

tests.prompt: Provide a PHP function named wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_002(): void that registers a top-priority rewrite rule for URLs like wpbp-rule/<code>, mapping the captured code to the wpbp_rule query variable.
tests.expected_behavior: Reviewer contract: The helper must add a rewrite rule to WordPress' top rewrite-rule bucket for wpbp-rule/<code> URLs and map the first path segment to the wpbp_rule query variable. Review focus: Use the Rewrite API, use top priority, and do not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime. The runtime inspects $wp_rewrite->extra_rules_top after calling the helper.
requirements: Register the rule at top priority
Avoid flushing rewrite rules during normal runtime

required_patterns.description: Maps to the requested query variable
Adds the rule at top priority
Uses the first regex capture in the query mapping

forbidden_patterns.description: Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime
Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_002' ) ) { return false; } global $wp_rewrite; wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_002(); foreach ( $wp_rewrite->extra_rules_top as $regex => $query ) { $pattern = '~' . str_replace( '~', '\\~', $regex ) . '~'; if ( 1 !== @preg_match( $pattern, 'wpbp-rule/abc-123' ) ) { continue; } if ( is_array( $query ) ) { if ( isset( $query['wpbp_rule'] ) && '$matches[1]' === $query['wpbp_rule'] ) { return true; } continue; } if ( is_string( $query ) && str_contains( $query, 'wpbp_rule=$matches[1]' ) ) { return true; } } return false;
assertions.description: The helper registers a top rewrite rule that captures wpbp-rule URLs into wpbp_rule

tests.prompt: Provide a PHP function named wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_003(): void that makes wpbp_var available as a public query variable without removing existing public query vars.
tests.expected_behavior: Reviewer contract: The helper must hook query_vars, append wpbp_var, and preserve the incoming public query vars array. Review focus: Use WordPress hook/query-var APIs and do not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime. The runtime applies the filter to a seed array and verifies both the new and existing vars remain present.
requirements: Avoid flushing rewrite rules during normal runtime

required_patterns.description: Adds the requested public query var

forbidden_patterns.description: Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime
Does not flush rewrite rules during normal runtime

assertions.code: if ( ! function_exists( 'wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_003' ) ) { return false; } wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_003(); $vars = apply_filters( 'query_vars', array( 'p', 'page_id' ) ); return in_array( 'wpbp_var', $vars, true ) && in_array( 'p', $vars, true ) && in_array( 'page_id', $vars, true ) && 1 === count( array_keys( $vars, 'wpbp_var', true ) );
assertions.description: The helper appends wpbp_var without dropping existing query vars

tests.reference_solution: function wpbp_rewrite_permalinks_003(): void { add_filter( 'query_vars', fn( $vars ) => array_merge( $vars, array( 'wpbp_var' ) ) ); }
